LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE LEGISLATURE MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT OF THE PLEADINGS IS ARGUED AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSISON BY JUDGE ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. COUNSEL FOR CALIFORNIS SATATE LEGISLTURE IS TO SUBMIT PROPROSED FORM OF ORDER TO BE EMAIL TO THE COURT AT CONTEST302TR@SFTC.ORG THAT IS VERBATIM TO THE TENTATIVE RULING AS WELL AS THE THE CITED RULINGS BROUGHT UP AT TODAYS HEARING. IF A REVIEW OF THE CITED CASES CANGES THE COURTS MIND WITH RESPECTC TO COURTS TENTATIVE RUING, THE COURT WILL GIVE ALL PARTIES NOTICE TO FURTHER BRIEFINGS. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN CSR6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Jan-23-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Ccp 438
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Ccp 438 -
Defendant Bay Area Toll Authority's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the second cause of action for invalidation of Regional Measure 3 is granted with 20 days leave to amend. Streets & Highways Code 30922 allows plaintiffs to bring an action to contest the validity of the toll increase authorized by Regional Measure 3. However, section 30922 does not expressly refer to the validation statues and thus the procedural requirements of the validation statutes do not apply. Government Code 50077.5 is inapplicable to the second cause of action since Regional Measure 3 was not approved per Article 3.5 which is the article referred to in section 50077.5. Because the second cause of action incorporates by reference paragraph 8 of the complaint which alleges that plaintiffs' lawsuit is a reverse validation action per CCP 863 and that allegation is incorrect, the second cause of action fails to state a claim. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to both of their causes of action to allege claims for declaratory relief, writ of mandate and/or relief per section 30922 to invalidate both SB 595 and Regional Measure 3. "An action for declaratory relief lies when the parties are in fundamental disagreement over the construction of particular legislation, or they dispute whether a public entity has engaged in conduct or established policies in violation of applicable law." (Alameda County Land Use Assn. v. City of Hayward (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1716, 1723.) "For every wrong there is a remedy." (Civil Code 3523.) BATA's argument that plaintiffs did not comply with the minimum notice period of the validation statutes is moot due to the court's determination that the validation statutes do not apply to Regional Measure 3. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 =3302/hk
